subtopics.name: Lattice energy and Born-Haber cycles
learning_outcomes: Define and use: enthalpy change of atomisation and lattice energy (gas phase ions to solid lattice) [cite: 7, 8, 9]
Define and use first electron affinity (EA) [cite: 10]
Explain factors affecting electron affinities [cite: 11]
Describe and explain trends in electron affinities for Group 16 and 17 [cite: 12]
Construct and use Born-Haber cycles for ionic solids (+1/+2 cations, -1/-2 anions) [cite: 13, 14]
Perform calculations involving Born-Haber cycles [cite: 15]
Explain qualitative effects of ionic charge and radius on lattice energy magnitude [cite: 16]

subtopics.name: Enthalpies of solution and hydration
learning_outcomes: Define and use enthalpy change of hydration and solution [cite: 20]
Construct and use energy cycles involving solution, lattice, and hydration enthalpies [cite: 21]
Carry out calculations involving these energy cycles [cite: 22]
Explain qualitative effects of ionic charge and radius on hydration enthalpy magnitude [cite: 23]

subtopics.name: Entropy change, ΔS
learning_outcomes: Define entropy (S) as the number of possible arrangements of particles and energy [cite: 30]
Predict and explain signs of entropy changes during state changes, temperature changes, and reactions affecting gaseous molecule numbers [cite: 31, 32, 33, 34]
Calculate reaction entropy change using standard entropies of reactants and products [cite: 35]

subtopics.name: Gibbs free energy change, ΔG
learning_outcomes: State and use the Gibbs equation: ΔG° = ΔH° – TΔS° [cite: 40]
Perform calculations using the Gibbs equation [cite: 41]
State process feasibility based on the sign of ΔG [cite: 42]
Predict the effect of temperature change on reaction feasibility [cite: 43]

subtopics.name: Electrolysis
learning_outcomes: Predict substances liberated based on state, redox series position, and concentration [cite: 49]
Apply the relationship F = Le [cite: 50]
Calculate charge passed (Q = It) and mass/volume of substances liberated [cite: 53, 54]
Describe determining the Avogadro constant via electrolytic methods [cite: 55]

subtopics.name: Standard electrode potentials, standard cell potentials and the Nernst equation
learning_outcomes: Define standard electrode (reduction) potential and standard cell potential [cite: 62, 63, 64]
Describe the standard hydrogen electrode [cite: 65]
Describe measurement methods for electrode potentials of metal/ions and ions in different oxidation states [cite: 67, 68, 69]
Calculate standard cell potential from two standard electrode potentials [cite: 70]
Use cell potentials to deduce polarity, electron flow, and reaction feasibility [cite: 71, 72, 73]
Deduce relative reactivity of species as oxidising or reducing agents [cite: 74]
Construct redox equations from half-equations [cite: 75]
Predict qualitatively and quantitatively (Nernst equation) how potential varies with ion concentration [cite: 76, 79, 80]
Understand and use ΔG° = –nE°cellF [cite: 82]

subtopics.name: Acids and bases
learning_outcomes: Use terms conjugate acid and conjugate base and identify pairs in reactions [cite: 91, 92]
Define mathematically and use pH, Ka, pKa, and Kw in calculations [cite: 93]
Calculate [H+(aq)] and pH for strong acids, strong alkalis, and weak acids [cite: 94, 95, 96, 97]
Define buffers, explain their formation and pH control (using equations), and describe uses (including blood pH) [cite: 98, 99, 100, 101]
Calculate buffer solution pH [cite: 102]
Understand, write expressions for, and calculate solubility product (Ksp) [cite: 103, 104, 105]
Understand and perform calculations for the common ion effect [cite: 106, 108]

subtopics.name: Partition coefficients
learning_outcomes: State meaning of partition coefficient (Kpc) [cite: 112]
Calculate and use Kpc for solutes in the same physical state in two solvents [cite: 113]
Understand factors affecting Kpc numerical value based on solute/solvent polarities [cite: 114]

subtopics.name: Simple rate equations, orders of reaction and rate constants
learning_outcomes: Explain and use terms: rate equation, order of reaction, rate constant, half-life, rate-determining step, and intermediate [cite: 123]
Use rate equations, deduce orders from graphs/experimental data, and calculate initial rates [cite: 125, 126, 127, 128]
Understand and use first-order reaction half-life (independent of concentration) in calculations [cite: 130, 131, 134]
Calculate rate constants using initial rates or half-life [cite: 132, 133, 134]
Suggest or deduce mechanisms, rate equations, and intermediates/catalysts for multi-step reactions [cite: 136, 138, 139, 140]
Describe qualitative effect of temperature change on rate constant and rate [cite: 141]

subtopics.name: Homogeneous and heterogeneous catalysts
learning_outcomes: Explain the difference between homogeneous and heterogeneous catalysts [cite: 145]
Describe mode of action for heterogeneous catalysts (adsorption, bond weakening, desorption) [cite: 146]
Describe mode of action for homogeneous catalysts (one step use, later step reformation) [cite: 149]

subtopics.name: Similarities and trends in the properties of the Group 2 metals and their compounds
learning_outcomes: Explain trends in thermal stability of nitrates and carbonates (polarisation effect) [cite: 161]
Explain variation in solubility and enthalpy change of solution for hydroxides and sulfates [cite: 162]

subtopics.name: General physical and chemical properties
learning_outcomes: Define transition elements (incomplete d orbitals in stable ions) [cite: 168]
Sketch 3dxy and 3dz2 orbitals [cite: 169]
Explain variable oxidation states, catalytic behavior, and complex ion formation [cite: 171, 172, 173, 175, 176, 177]

subtopics.name: General characteristic chemical properties
learning_outcomes: Describe complex formation with ligands (water, ammonia, hydroxide, chloride) [cite: 186]
Define ligands (mono-, bi-, polydentate) and complexes [cite: 187, 190, 191, 192]
Describe complex geometries and predict formulas/charges [cite: 193, 195]
Explain ligand exchange and predict redox reaction feasibility using E° [cite: 196, 197]
Perform calculations for specific redox systems (manganate/oxalate, etc.) [cite: 199, 200, 201]

subtopics.name: Colour of complexes
learning_outcomes: Define degenerate and non-degenerate d orbitals [cite: 206]
Describe d orbital splitting in octahedral and tetrahedral complexes [cite: 208, 209]
Explain color based on light absorption during electron promotion [cite: 210]
Describe effects of different ligands on splitting magnitude and observed color [cite: 211]

subtopics.name: Stereoisomerism in transition element complexes
learning_outcomes: Describe geometrical (cis/trans) and optical isomerism in specific complexes [cite: 220, 221]
Deduce overall polarity of complexes [cite: 222]

subtopics.name: Stability constants, Kstab
learning_outcomes: Define stability constant (Kstab) [cite: 226]
Write Kstab expressions and perform calculations [cite: 227, 228]
Explain ligand exchange in terms of Kstab values [cite: 229]

topic_name: An introduction to A Level organic chemistry

subtopics.name: Formulas, functional groups and naming
learning_outcomes: Interpret and use general, structural, displayed, and skeletal formulas [cite: 246]
Use systematic nomenclature for aliphatic (up to C6) and simple aromatic molecules [cite: 247, 248]

subtopics.name: Characteristic organic reactions
learning_outcomes: Understand terminology: electrophilic substitution and addition-elimination [cite: 252, 253]

subtopics.name: Shapes of aromatic organic molecules; σ and π bonds
learning_outcomes: Describe/explain the shape of benzene (sp2 hybridisation, delocalised π system) [cite: 257]

subtopics.name: Isomerism: optical
learning_outcomes: Understand enantiomer properties, optical activity, and racemic mixtures [cite: 261, 263]
Explain chirality relevance to drug synthesis (biological activity, separation, chiral catalysts) [cite: 265, 266, 267, 268]

subtopics.name: Arenes
learning_outcomes: Describe reactions: substitution (Cl2/Br2), nitration, Friedel-Crafts alkylation/acylation, side-chain oxidation, hydrogenation [cite: 279, 280, 281, 282, 283, 284, 285]
Describe electrophilic substitution mechanisms and effect of delocalisation [cite: 285, 286, 287]
Predict halogenation position and understand directing effects of substituents [cite: 288, 289]

subtopics.name: Halogen compounds
learning_outcomes: Recall halogenoarene production [cite: 294]
Explain reactivity differences between halogenoalkanes and halogenoarenes [cite: 295]

subtopics.name: Alcohols
learning_outcomes: Describe reaction with acyl chlorides to form esters [cite: 303]

subtopics.name: Phenol
learning_outcomes: Recall phenol production from phenylamine (diazonium salt route) [cite: 307, 308, 309]
Describe phenol chemistry: bases, Na, diazonium salts (azo), nitration, bromination [cite: 310, 311, 312, 313, 314, 315]
Explain/compare acidities (water, phenol, ethanol) [cite: 316, 317]
Explain directing effects and reagent differences vs benzene [cite: 318, 319]

subtopics.name: Carboxylic acids
learning_outcomes: Recall benzoic acid production [cite: 325]
Describe acyl chloride formation using PCl3, PCl5, or SOCl2 [cite: 327]
Describe oxidation of methanoic and ethanedioic acids [cite: 329, 330]
Explain relative acidities of carboxylic acids, phenols, alcohols, and Cl-substituted acids [cite: 331, 332]

subtopics.name: Esters
learning_outcomes: Recall ester production from alcohols and acyl chlorides [cite: 339, 340]

subtopics.name: Acyl chlorides
learning_outcomes: Recall production methods [cite: 344, 345]
Describe reactions: hydrolysis, with alcohols, phenols, ammonia, and amines [cite: 346, 347, 348, 349, 350, 351]
Describe addition-elimination mechanism [cite: 352]
Explain relative ease of hydrolysis vs alkyl/aryl chlorides [cite: 353]

subtopics.name: Primary and secondary amines
learning_outcomes: Recall production from halogenoalkanes, amides, and nitriles [cite: 358, 359, 360, 361]
Describe condensation with acyl chlorides and explain basicity [cite: 362, 363]

subtopics.name: Phenylamine and azo compounds
learning_outcomes: Describe phenylamine preparation and its reactions (Br2, diazonium salt) [cite: 370, 372, 373]
Explain relative basicities (ammonia, ethylamine, phenylamine) [cite: 375]
Recall azo coupling, identifying the azo group, and dye uses [cite: 376, 377, 378]

subtopics.name: Amides
learning_outcomes: Recall production from acyl chlorides [cite: 383, 384, 385]
Describe hydrolysis and reduction [cite: 387, 388]
Explain why amides are weaker bases than amines [cite: 389]

subtopics.name: Amino acids
learning_outcomes: Describe zwitterion formation and isoelectric point [cite: 393]
Describe peptide bond formation and predict electrophoresis results [cite: 394, 395]

subtopics.name: Condensation polymerisation
learning_outcomes: Describe formation of polyesters and polyamides [cite: 403, 406]
Deduce repeat units and identify monomers [cite: 410, 411]

subtopics.name: Predicting the type of polymerisation
learning_outcomes: Predict/deduce polymerisation type for monomers or sections [cite: 415, 416]

subtopics.name: Degradable polymers
learning_outcomes: Recognise poly(alkenes) inertness and biodegradability of polyesters/polyamides [cite: 420, 422]

subtopics.name: Organic synthesis
learning_outcomes: Identify functional groups and predict reactions [cite: 427, 428]
Devise multi-step synthetic routes and analyse given routes [cite: 429]

subtopics.name: Thin-layer chromatography
learning_outcomes: Understand/interpret stationary phase, mobile phase, Rf values, and solubility factors [cite: 438, 441, 443, 444]

subtopics.name: Gas/liquid chromatography
learning_outcomes: Understand/interpret stationary/mobile phases, retention time, and chromatograms [cite: 448, 451, 452, 453]

subtopics.name: Carbon-13 NMR spectroscopy
learning_outcomes: Analyse spectra to deduce carbon environments and possible structures [cite: 457, 458, 459]
Predict number of peaks [cite: 460]

subtopics.name: Proton (H) NMR spectroscopy
learning_outcomes: Analyse chemical shift, peak area, and splitting patterns (n+1 rule) [cite: 468, 469, 470, 471, 472]
Predict shifts and splitting; use of TMS and deuterated solvents [cite: 474, 475, 476]
Identify O-H/N-H protons using D2O exchange [cite: 477]
